findings.title: Empty / uninstrumented signup funnel is the #1 blocker to the 100/day north star
findings.founder_problem: There is no measurable acquisition happening. Without instrumented top-of-funnel events and a public value moment, no experiment can be evaluated against the 100/day target.
findings.investor_problem: Investor side is not being acquired at all; no investor signup operations recorded.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: signup_velocity.signups_per_day=0, gap_to_target=100, pct_of_target=0; ai_logs had 0 of [url_submitted, login_completed, lookup_signup_completed, checkout_completed] in 7d; internal_events.sampled=0.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Ship a public, pre-signup 'instant investor match preview' so a visitor gets value (a ranked shortlist + one thesis-fit reason) BEFORE creating an account, then instrument every funnel stage. This is the single lever that converts the 0 baseline into measurable signups.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Public URL-submission -> instant match preview -> account-create CTA. Instrument awareness/value_preview/account_created events in ai_logs.

handoff.title: Public instant investor-match preview (value before signup) + funnel instrumentation

findings.title: Investors are drowning in 'vibe-coded slop' — dealflow volume is now a vanity metric
findings.founder_problem: Quality founders are buried under noise and struggle to stand out / earn investor trust.
findings.investor_problem: AI lets software startups fabricate credibility in an afternoon; investors face a surge of low-signal submissions and fewer seed startups graduating to Series A, so they concentrate capital in fewer, stronger bets and need better filtering.

evidence.rss: Sifted (2026-06): 'Investors bemoan vibe-coded product slop'; Crunchbase News: 'AI Is Rewriting What Investors Should Look For In Early Startup Teams'.
evidence.web: Crunchbase/seed reports 2026: 'dealflow volume has become a vanity metric... software startups can fabricate credibility in a single afternoon'; fewer seed->Series A graduations; capital concentrating.

findings.pythh_opportunity: A data-completeness + quality/traction signal score on every startup profile, powering a curated weekly dealflow digest for investors. Opens the investor side of the marketplace (currently 0 acquisition).
findings.suggested_product_shape: Profile 'data completeness score' + signal filter; investor-facing curated digest of high-signal matches.

handoff.title: Data-completeness/quality score + curated investor dealflow digest

findings.title: Founders burn ~6 weeks on a spreadsheet + a month cold-emailing wrong-fit investors
findings.founder_problem: Most founders spend more time finding investors than talking to them: ~6 weeks building a list, ~1 month emailing people who were never going to say yes, with work split across disconnected tools (inboxes, sheets, notes, calendars).
findings.investor_problem: Mis-targeted cold outreach adds to inbound noise (links to F-2026-0622-02).

evidence.web: seedlegals/thevccorner 2026: 'most founders spend more time looking for investors than talking to them... six weeks building a spreadsheet, another month cold emailing people who were never going to say yes'; 'work split across too many disconnected tools'.
evidence.rss: Sifted (2026-06): fintech founders crowdfunded EUR8m in <1hr — founders routing around painful VC outreach via alternative channels.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Pythh's core wedge: instant match preview + 'why this VC' thesis-fit explanation that replaces the spreadsheet-and-cold-email grind with a ranked, reasoned shortlist in minutes.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Match preview returns ranked investors each with a one-line thesis-fit rationale and suggested approach.

handoff.title: Thesis-fit explainer ('why this VC') on every match

findings.title: Targeting must be partner-level, not firm-level — a data gap competitors leave open
findings.founder_problem: Every partner has distinct tastes and convictions; founders waste meetings pitching firms instead of the one aligned partner, and lack partner-level data to do it.
findings.investor_problem: Individual partners get pitched on themes outside their conviction.

evidence.web: thevccorner 2026: 'partner targeting — not firm targeting'; seedblink 'what investors expect from founders in 2026'.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Resolve and score thesis fit at partner granularity (not firm), a differentiator vs firm-level databases (PitchBook, Harmonic). Feeds the match preview and completeness score.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Partner-level profiles with thesis/stage/check-size completeness; match at partner level.

findings.title: Fresh seed dry powder + bifurcated AI-winner market = deployment-timing matching opportunity
findings.founder_problem: In a bifurcated, AI-winner-take-most market founders must time outreach to funds that just raised / are actively deploying, and broaden capital sources.
findings.investor_problem: Newly-raised funds (e.g. Seedcamp $320M) need quality seed dealflow to deploy.

evidence.rss: TechCrunch/Crunchbase/Sifted (2026-06): 'Seedcamp raises $320M new fund'; Crunchbase 'Robotics startups on fire... record numbers 2026'.
evidence.web: Crunchbase: Q1 2026 record ~$297B venture, 81% to AI; Morgan Stanley: >half of founders pursuing broader range of capital sources.

findings.pythh_opportunity: Investor deployment-timing alerts ('this fund just closed / is actively deploying in your sector') for founders, and broader-capital-source matching. Recurring alerts create a retention loop.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Timing signal on investor profiles ('recently raised / actively deploying') surfaced in matches + alert subscription.

findings.title: Valuation/terms uncertainty in a frothy-but-cautious market — founders don't know what to ask, investors lack live comps
findings.founder_problem: Founders entering a round have no trustworthy, sector/stage-specific reference for what valuation, check size, or terms (SAFE cap, dilution) to expect — so they over/under-ask, stall, or get washed out in negotiation.
findings.investor_problem: Investors want defensible, current comps to anchor offers; firm-level databases lag and headline mega-rounds distort expectations.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: crunchbase_news friction hit category=pricing_terms on 'AppsFlyer Reportedly Lands $1B At $2.7B Valuation'; Crunchbase News: 'Greenspan Penned Irrational Exuberance 30 Years Ago. It Aged Well.' (valuation-frothiness caution); Q1 2026 record ~$297B venture with 81% to AI = bifurcated, distorted pricing.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external/secondary); pricing_terms is a recurring friction category with a live RSS hit today

findings.pythh_opportunity: Add a valuation/terms benchmark to the match preview: 'comparable raises at your stage/sector look like X' plus a thesis-aligned check-size expectation per matched investor. Appeals to both sides and deepens the value moment before signup.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Stage/sector terms benchmark widget in match preview + per-investor expected check size; sourced from internal startup_events + funding RSS.

findings.title: 'Services-as-software' shift — position Pythh as an agent that does the fundraising work, not another database
findings.founder_problem: Founders are tired of buying yet another data tool they must operate manually (lists, CRMs, spreadsheets); they want the work done for them.
findings.investor_problem: Investors equally want curated output (a digest, a shortlist) rather than another dashboard to maintain.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: crunchbase_news 'Saas Isn't Coming Back. Something Much Bigger Is Replacing It' (services-as-software / agentic shift); venturebeat cluster on agentic enterprises and self-improving agents.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external); positioning/messaging signal rather than a discrete feature gap

findings.pythh_opportunity: Frame Pythh's wedge (instant match preview + thesis-fit explainer + curated digest) as agentic 'fundraising-as-a-service' in landing copy and onboarding — differentiator vs database competitors (PitchBook, Harmonic, Affinity) and an awareness lever.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Messaging/positioning experiment for Growth Agent: 'Pythh does the fundraising legwork' landing variant; measure value_preview->account_created lift.

findings.title: Founders are routing around VC — matching to the right capital TYPE (not just VCs) broadens Pythh's TAM
findings.founder_problem: Many founders are not VC-fit (wrong stage, sector, growth profile) yet still need capital. They increasingly bypass the VC grind via crowdfunding, angels, revenue-based finance, and strategics — but have no single place that tells them which capital TYPE fits them and routes them to it.
findings.investor_problem: Non-VC capital providers (syndicates, crowdfunding platforms, RBF, angels) also lack a quality, pre-qualified founder pipeline.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: Sifted (Jun 2026) 'How these fintech founders crowdfunded EUR8m in less than an hour' — founders raising fast outside traditional VC outreach.
evidence.web: Morgan Stanley 2026: >half of founders pursuing a broader range of capital sources; prior-cycle corroboration.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external/secondary); recurring 'broader capital sources' theme across multiple cycles

findings.pythh_opportunity: Add a capital-type router to the match preview: classify the founder and surface the best-fit capital TYPE (VC / angel / crowdfunding / RBF / strategic) alongside specific matches. Captures the large segment of founders who would bounce off a VC-only product, widening top-of-funnel without new acquisition spend.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Match preview returns a 'best capital fit' verdict + routes (VC shortlist, or angel/crowdfunding/RBF alternatives) instead of VC-only results.

handoff.title: Capital-type router in match preview (VC vs angel/crowdfunding/RBF/strategic)

findings.title: Capital is rotating fast between hot sectors — a live 'is your sector hot / who's deploying' signal sharpens the value moment
findings.founder_problem: Founders can't see, in the moment, whether capital is flowing into their sector this quarter or which investors are actively writing checks there — so they mistime outreach and pitch funds that have rotated away.
findings.investor_problem: Investors want to spot where momentum is concentrating (and where it is cooling) without manually tracking round-by-round news.

evidence.rss: research-snapshot 2026-06-22: Crunchbase News 'Robotics Startups On Fire As Venture Funding Surges To Record Numbers In 2026'; 'World-Model Startup Odyssey Leads With $310M In Slower Week For Large Deals' (rotation + cooling in same week); ~81% of Q1 2026 venture to AI.
evidence.strength: hypothesis (external); distinct from F-05 (fund-level deploy timing) — this is sector-level demand heat

findings.pythh_opportunity: A sector-heat signal on the match preview: 'Your sector is hot right now — N matched investors made a check here in the last 90 days' (or a cooling warning). Cheap to compute from funding RSS + internal startup_events; deepens the pre-signup value moment for both audiences and gives investors a reason to return.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Sector-heat badge in preview (heating/cooling + count of recently-active matched investors), sourced from funding RSS + internal startup_events.

findings.title: Internal founder-side inventory is empty (startup_events = 0) — the match engine has nothing to match
findings.founder_problem: No founder/startup events are being captured, so even a perfect match preview has no live demand-side data to learn from or personalize against.
findings.investor_problem: Without a populated founder/startup inventory there is no dealflow to curate or digest for investors — the investor side cannot launch.

evidence.internal: research-snapshot 2026-06-22T22:20Z: internal_events.sampled=0, friction_hits=0; startup_events returned 0 rows over the 7-day window. Pairs with F-01 (uninstrumented funnel).
evidence.strength: validated (internal data, strongest evidence) — this is a confirmed supply gap, not a hypothesis

findings.pythh_opportunity: Seed and instrument the founder-supply layer: capture every URL submission / preview request as a startup_event so the match engine and dealflow digest have inventory. This is the data prerequisite that makes instant_match_preview and dealflow_quality_score actually work end-to-end.
findings.suggested_product_shape: Log startup_events on every public preview request (URL, derived profile, sector, stage); backfill from existing sources so matching + digest have a corpus.
findings.signup_lever: conversion (enables match quality) + investor retention (enables digest)

handoff.title: Capture startup_events on every preview request to populate match-engine inventory
